name: Yuvabharathi International School
tagline: Affordable CBSE and Cambridge education in Singapore's west
description: Yuvabharathi International School (YBIS) is one of Singapore's most affordable international schools, located in Jurong West. Established in 2009, the school serves approximately 1,300 students and offers three distinct curriculum pathways: CBSE (India's Central Board of Secondary Education) from primary through Class 12, the Cambridge International programme (Primary through A-Level), and a Montessori early years programme. YBIS operates on an April-to-March academic year and provides a vegetarian-only campus reflecting its Indian heritage. The school's expansive facilities include science labs, a performing arts theatre, and multiple sports fields supporting cricket, football, and basketball.
address: 3 Hu Ching Road, Jurong West, Singapore 619651

admission.process: Submit an online application with academic records. Students sit for a grade-level assessment in English and Mathematics, and families meet with the academic team for an interaction.
admission.deadlines: Admissions open November-December. Academic year runs April to March. Mid-term admissions considered on a case-by-case basis.

faqs.question: Does YBIS offer both CBSE and Cambridge?
faqs.answer: Yes, Yuvabharathi is one of the few schools in Singapore offering both the CBSE Indian curriculum and the Cambridge International pathway, giving families the flexibility to choose the qualification that best suits their child's future plans.

faqs.question: Is the campus fully vegetarian?
faqs.answer: Yes, in keeping with the school's Indian cultural values, the on-site cafeteria serves vegetarian meals only. Students are welcome to bring their own food if preferred.

seo.title: Yuvabharathi International School Singapore — CBSE & Cambridge Dual Curriculum | EduAtlas
seo.description: Yuvabharathi International School in Jurong West: Affordable CBSE and Cambridge education from SGD 6,000/year. 1,300 students, Montessori to A-Level. View 2026 fees and admissions.
